response.justification: The candidate has extensive experience in software development, including C and C++ in a relevant role at National Instruments, but lacks recent, deep, or focused experience in C++-centric industrial automation, secure development lifecycle (SDL), or IEC 62443 standards. Their expertise is heavily skewed toward web, mobile, and embedded systems (Node.js, TypeScript, D, JavaScript), with limited exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA) or cloud platforms (Azure). Recent roles emphasize full-stack development rather than core C++ systems design and cybersecurity for industrial solutions.
strengths: Strong foundational C/C++ experience from National Instruments role, including component development for engineering software (LabWindows™/CVI).
Demonstrated ability to work in high-performance, low-latency environments (e.g., micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby).
Experience with secure development practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentorship (refactoring dojos, code katas).
concerns: Limited recent or direct experience with C++ in industrial automation, STL, or message-broker systems (key requirements for the role).
No visible exposure to IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial cybersecurity standards in recent roles or projects.
Lack of cloud platform expertise (e.g., Azure) and industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA), which are advantageous for the role.

key_factors.factor: Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and IEC 62443 compliance
Industrial automation protocols (Modbus, OPC UA) and cloud exposure
